title: King and Pawn vs King
summary: The most common endgame — master it

steps.title: The Core Endgame
steps.text: Every chess player must know K+P vs K cold. The result depends on ONE thing: can the stronger side get his king IN FRONT of the pawn with opposition? If yes, the pawn promotes. If no, it's a draw.

steps.title: Key Squares
steps.text: For a pawn on the 5th rank or lower, the "key squares" are the three squares diagonally ahead of it. If your king reaches any one, you win. Learn the key squares for each pawn position.

steps.hint: Kd6 or Kf6 both grab the opposition.
